author: Her

capabilities.description: Open the embedded terminal drawer (a real login shell with full TUI support) and return the current screen.
capabilities.title: Open terminal

capabilities.description: Read the terminal's currently visible screen — works for shell output and full-screen TUIs (vim, claude, htop). Call after terminal.send to see how the program reacted.
capabilities.title: Read terminal screen

capabilities.description: Type into the terminal exactly like a user: literal text, an optional named special key, and optional enter. Examples: run a command with {text: "claude", enter: true}; answer a TUI prompt with {text: "yes", enter: true}; navigate a TUI with {key: "down"} then {key: "enter"}; interrupt with {key: "ctrl-c"}. The reply includes the refreshed screen.

enter.description: Press Enter after the text (default false).

key.description: Named special key sent after text: enter, tab, escape, backspace, space, up, down, left, right, ctrl-c, ctrl-d, ctrl-z, ctrl-r, ctrl-l, shift-tab.

capabilities.title: Type into terminal

description: An embedded terminal the conversation can drive: open it, read the visible screen (including TUIs), and type input with user approval.
name: Terminal Bridge
systemPromptAddendum: The embedded terminal is a real login shell with full TUI emulation. To work with it: terminal.open shows it and returns the screen; terminal.send types like a user (every send requires approval and surfaces the drawer so the user watches); terminal.read re-reads the screen. For long-running commands or TUIs (like claude), send input, then read the screen again after a moment to see the result; repeat read if the program is still working. Never chain destructive shell commands without explaining them to the user first.
